## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flanders&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Culture")]

Main articles: [Dutch language](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"), [Flemish dialects](/wiki/Flemish_dialects "Flemish dialects"), [Flemish people](/wiki/Flemish_people "Flemish people"), and [Flemish Movement](/wiki/Flemish_Movement "Flemish Movement")

### Language and literature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flanders&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Language and literature")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Guidogezelle.jpg/250px-Guidogezelle.jpg)](/wiki/File:Guidogezelle.jpg)

Statue of [Gezelle](/wiki/Guido_Gezelle "Guido Gezelle") in [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), by sculptor [Jules Lagae](/wiki/Jules_Lagae "Jules Lagae")

The standard language in Flanders is [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"); spelling and grammar are regulated by a single authority, the [Dutch Language Union](/wiki/Dutch_Language_Union "Dutch Language Union") (*Nederlandse Taalunie*), comprising a committee of ministers of the Flemish and Dutch governments, their advisory council of appointed experts, a controlling commission of 22 parliamentarians, and a secretariate.[[45]](#cite_note-Taalunie1-51)[[46]](#cite_note-Taalunie2-52) The term [Flemish](/wiki/Flemish_dialects "Flemish dialects") can be applied to the Dutch spoken in Flanders; it shows many regional and local variations.[[47]](#cite_note-53)

The earliest example of literature in non-[standardized dialects](/wiki/Standardized_dialect "Standardized dialect") in the current area of Flanders is [Hendrik van Veldeke](/wiki/Hendrik_van_Veldeke "Hendrik van Veldeke")'s *Eneas Romance*, the first courtly romance in a [Germanic language](/wiki/Germanic_language "Germanic language") (12th century). With a writer of [Hendrik Conscience](/wiki/Hendrik_Conscience "Hendrik Conscience")'s stature, [Flemish literature](/wiki/Flemish_literature "Flemish literature") rose ahead of French literature in Belgium's early history.[[48]](#cite_note-Letterkundig-Museum-54)[[49]](#cite_note-Couttenier-55) [Guido Gezelle](/wiki/Guido_Gezelle "Guido Gezelle") not only explicitly referred to his writings as Flemish but used it in many of his poems, and strongly defended it:

**Original** from *kleengedichtjes* (1860?)[[50]](#cite_note-kleengedichtjes1-56)[[51]](#cite_note-kleengedichtjes2-57)

> Gij zegt dat 't vlaamsch te niet zal gaan:  
> 't en zal!  
> dat 't waalsch gezwets zal boven slaan:  
> 't en zal!  
> Dat hopen, dat begeren wij:  
> dat zeggen en dat zweren wij:  
> zoo lange als wij ons weren, wij:  
> 't en zal, 't en zal,  
> 't en zal!

Translation

> You say Flemish will fade away:  
> It shan't!  
> that Walloon twaddle will have its way:  
> It shan't!  
> This we hope, for this we hanker:  
> this we say and this we vow:  
> as long as we fight back, we:  
> It shan't, It shan't,  
> It shan't!

The distinction between [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_literature "Dutch literature") and Flemish literature, often perceived politically, is also made on intrinsic grounds by some experts such as Kris Humbeeck, professor of literature at the [University of Antwerp](/wiki/University_of_Antwerp "University of Antwerp").[[52]](#cite_note-de-Ridder-58)[[53]](#cite_note-Humbeeck-59) Nevertheless, most [Dutch-language](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language") literature read (and appreciated to varying degrees) in Flanders is the same as that in the Netherlands.[[54]](#cite_note-60)

Influential Flemish writers include [Ernest Claes](/wiki/Ernest_Claes "Ernest Claes"), [Stijn Streuvels](/wiki/Stijn_Streuvels "Stijn Streuvels") and [Felix Timmermans](/wiki/Felix_Timmermans "Felix Timmermans"). Their novels mostly describe rural life in Flanders in the 19th century and at beginning of the 20th. Widely read by the older generations, they are considered somewhat old-fashioned by present-day critics. Some famous Flemish writers of the early 20th century wrote in French, including Nobel Prize winners (1911) [Maurice Maeterlinck](/wiki/Maurice_Maeterlinck "Maurice Maeterlinck") and [Emile Verhaeren](/wiki/Emile_Verhaeren "Emile Verhaeren"). They were followed by a younger generation, including [Paul van Ostaijen](/wiki/Paul_van_Ostaijen "Paul van Ostaijen") and [Gaston Burssens](/wiki/Gaston_Burssens "Gaston Burssens"), who *[activated](/wiki/Activism "Activism")* the [Flemish Movement](/wiki/Flemish_Movement "Flemish Movement").[[52]](#cite_note-de-Ridder-58) Still widely read and translated into other languages (including English) are the novels of authors such as [Willem Elsschot](/wiki/Willem_Elsschot "Willem Elsschot"), [Louis Paul Boon](/wiki/Louis_Paul_Boon "Louis Paul Boon") and [Hugo Claus](/wiki/Hugo_Claus "Hugo Claus"). The recent crop of writers includes the novelists [Tom Lanoye](/wiki/Tom_Lanoye "Tom Lanoye") and [Herman Brusselmans](/wiki/Herman_Brusselmans "Herman Brusselmans"), and poets such as the married couple [Herman de Coninck](/wiki/Herman_de_Coninck "Herman de Coninck") and [Kristien Hemmerechts](/wiki/Kristien_Hemmerechts "Kristien Hemmerechts").

#### Languages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flanders&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Languages")]

At the creation of the Belgian state, French was the only official language. Historically Flanders was a Dutch-speaking region. For a long period, French was used as a [second language](/wiki/Second_language "Second language") and, like elsewhere in Europe, commonly spoken among the aristocracy. There is still a French-speaking minority in Flanders, especially in the [municipalities with language facilities](/wiki/Municipalities_with_language_facilities "Municipalities with language facilities"), along the language border and the [Brussels periphery](/wiki/Municipalities_with_language_facilities#Rim_municipalities "Municipalities with language facilities") (Vlaamse Rand), though many of them are French-speakers that migrated to Flanders in recent decades.

In [French Flanders](/wiki/French_Flanders "French Flanders"), French is the only official language and now the native language of the majority of the population, but there is still a minority of Dutch-speakers living there. French is also the primary language in the officially bilingual [Brussels Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels_Capital_Region "Brussels Capital Region") (see [Francization of Brussels](/wiki/Francization_of_Brussels "Francization of Brussels")).

Many Flemings are also able to speak French, children in Flanders generally get their first French lessons in the 5th primary year (normally around 10 years). But the current lack of French outside the educational context makes it hard to maintain a decent level of French. As such, the proficiency of French is declining. Flemish pupils are also obligated to follow English lessons as their third language. Normally from the second secondary year (around 14 years old), but the ubiquity of English in movies, music, IT and even advertisements makes it easier to learn and maintain the English language.

The public radio and television broadcaster in Flanders is [VRT](/wiki/Vlaamse_Radio-_en_Televisieomroeporganisatie "Vlaamse Radio- en Televisieomroeporganisatie"), which operates the TV channels [VRT 1](/wiki/VRT_1 "VRT 1"), [VRT Canvas](/wiki/Canvas_(Belgium) "Canvas (Belgium)"), [Ketnet](/wiki/Ketnet "Ketnet"), and (together with the Netherlands) [BVN](/wiki/BVN "BVN"). Flemish provinces each have up to two TV channels as well. Commercial television broadcasters include [vtm](/wiki/VTM_(TV_channel) "VTM (TV channel)") and [Vier](/wiki/Vier "Vier") (VT4). Popular TV series are for example *[Thuis](/wiki/Thuis "Thuis")* and *[F.C. De Kampioenen](/wiki/F.C._De_Kampioenen "F.C. De Kampioenen")*.

The five most successful Flemish films were *[Loft](/wiki/Loft_(2008_film) "Loft (2008 film)")* (2008; 1,186,071 visitors), *[Koko Flanel](/wiki/Koko_Flanel "Koko Flanel")* (1990; 1,082,000 tickets sold), *[Hector](/wiki/Hector_(1987_film) "Hector (1987 film)")* (1987; 933,000 tickets sold), *[Daens](/wiki/Daens_(film) "Daens (film)")* (1993; 848,000 tickets sold) and *[De Zaak Alzheimer](/wiki/The_Alzheimer_Case "The Alzheimer Case")* (2003; 750,000 tickets sold). The first and last ones were directed by [Erik Van Looy](/wiki/Erik_Van_Looy "Erik Van Looy"), and an American remake is being made of both of them, respectively *[The Loft](/wiki/The_Loft_(2012_film) "The Loft (2012 film)")* (2012) and *[The Memory of a Killer](/wiki/The_Memory_of_a_Killer "The Memory of a Killer")*. The other three ones were directed by [Stijn Coninx](/wiki/Stijn_Coninx "Stijn Coninx").

Newspapers are grouped under three main publishers: [De Persgroep](/wiki/De_Persgroep "De Persgroep") with *[Het Laatste Nieuws](/wiki/Het_Laatste_Nieuws "Het Laatste Nieuws")*, the most popular newspaper in Flanders, *[De Morgen](/wiki/De_Morgen "De Morgen")* and *[De Tijd](/wiki/De_Tijd "De Tijd")*. Then [Corelio](/wiki/Corelio "Corelio") with *[De Gentenaar](/w/index.php?title=De_Gentenaar&action=edit&redlink=1 "De Gentenaar (page does not exist)") [[nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/De_Gentenaar "nl:De Gentenaar")]*, the oldest extant Flemish newspaper, *[Het Nieuwsblad](/wiki/Het_Nieuwsblad "Het Nieuwsblad")* and *[De Standaard](/wiki/De_Standaard "De Standaard")*. Lastly, [Concentra](/wiki/Concentra "Concentra") publishes *[Gazet van Antwerpen](/wiki/Gazet_van_Antwerpen "Gazet van Antwerpen")* and *[Het Belang van Limburg](/wiki/Het_Belang_van_Limburg "Het Belang van Limburg")*.

Magazines include *[Knack](/wiki/Knack_(magazine) "Knack (magazine)")* and *[HUMO](/wiki/HUMO "HUMO")*.

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flanders&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Sports")]

Further information: [Sport in Belgium](/wiki/Sport_in_Belgium "Sport in Belgium")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Kim_Clijsters_2006.jpg/250px-Kim_Clijsters_2006.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kim_Clijsters_2006.jpg)

Kim Clijsters was [WTA Player of the Year](/wiki/WTA_Awards "WTA Awards") in 2005 and 2010

Association football (soccer) is one of the most popular sports in both parts of Belgium, together with cycling, tennis, swimming and judo.[[55]](#cite_note-61)

In cycling, the [Tour of Flanders](/wiki/Tour_of_Flanders_(men%27s_race) "Tour of Flanders (men's race)") is considered one of the five "[Monuments](/wiki/Classic_cycle_races "Classic cycle races")". Other "[Flanders Classics](/wiki/Flanders_Classics "Flanders Classics")" races include *[Dwars door Vlaanderen](/wiki/Dwars_door_Vlaanderen "Dwars door Vlaanderen")* and [Gent–Wevelgem](/wiki/Gent%E2%80%93Wevelgem "Gent–Wevelgem"). [Eddy Merckx](/wiki/Eddy_Merckx "Eddy Merckx") is widely regarded as the greatest cyclist of all time, with five victories in the [Tour de France](/wiki/Tour_de_France "Tour de France") and numerous other cycling records.[[56]](#cite_note-62) His hour speed record (set in 1972) stood for 12 years.

[Jean-Marie Pfaff](/wiki/Jean-Marie_Pfaff "Jean-Marie Pfaff"), a former Belgian goalkeeper, is considered one of the greatest in the history of football (soccer).[[57]](#cite_note-63)

[Kim Clijsters](/wiki/Kim_Clijsters "Kim Clijsters") (as well as the French-speaking Belgian [Justine Henin](/wiki/Justine_Henin "Justine Henin")) was [Player of the Year](/wiki/WTA_Awards "WTA Awards") twice in the [Women's Tennis Association](/wiki/Women%27s_Tennis_Association "Women's Tennis Association") as she was ranked the number one female tennis player.

[Kim Gevaert](/wiki/Kim_Gevaert "Kim Gevaert") and [Tia Hellebaut](/wiki/Tia_Hellebaut "Tia Hellebaut") are notable track and field stars from Flanders.

The [1920 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1920_Summer_Olympics "1920 Summer Olympics") were held in Antwerp. [Jacques Rogge](/wiki/Jacques_Rogge "Jacques Rogge") was president of the [International Olympic Committee](/wiki/International_Olympic_Committee "International Olympic Committee") from 2001 to 2013.

The Flemish government agency for sports is [Bloso](/wiki/Bloso "Bloso").

### Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flanders&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Music")]

Further information: [Music of Belgium](/wiki/Music_of_Belgium "Music of Belgium")

Flanders is known for its music festivals, like the annual [Rock Werchter](/wiki/Rock_Werchter "Rock Werchter"), [Tomorrowland](/wiki/Tomorrowland_(festival) "Tomorrowland (festival)") and [Pukkelpop](/wiki/Pukkelpop "Pukkelpop"). The [Gentse Feesten](/wiki/Gentse_Feesten "Gentse Feesten") is another very large yearly event.

The best-selling Flemish group or artist is the (Flemish-Dutch) group [2 Unlimited](/wiki/2_Unlimited "2 Unlimited"), followed by (Italian-born) [Rocco Granata](/wiki/Rocco_Granata "Rocco Granata"), [Technotronic](/wiki/Technotronic "Technotronic"), [Helmut Lotti](/wiki/Helmut_Lotti "Helmut Lotti") and [Vaya Con Dios](/wiki/Vaya_Con_Dios_(band) "Vaya Con Dios (band)").

The weekly charts of best-selling singles is the [Ultratop 50](/wiki/Ultratop_50 "Ultratop 50"). "Kvraagetaan" by the [Fixkes](/wiki/Fixkes "Fixkes") holds the current record for longest time at No. 1 on the chart.

1. **[^](#cite_ref-fn1_4-0)** French: *Flandre* [[flɑ̃dʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Fr-fr-paris-Flandre.ogg "File:Fr-fr-paris-Flandre.ogg").
2. **[^](#cite_ref-fn2_9-0)** Only about 8% of Brussels inhabitants identify as Flemish, while the rest identify as French-speaking or non-Belgian.[[6]](#cite_note-BruS13EN-7)[[7]](#cite_note-8)
3. **[^](#cite_ref-fn3_10-0)** Although this original meaning is still relevant, in modern times the term "Flanders" came to refer to a larger area, and is used to refer to the entire Dutch-speaking part of Belgium, stretching all the way to the [Meuse](/wiki/Meuse "Meuse"), as well as cultural movements such as [Flemish art](/wiki/Flemish_art "Flemish art").
4. **[^](#cite_ref-19)** An *Antverpian*, derived from *Antverpia*, the Latin name of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"), is an inhabitant of this city; the term is also the [adjective](/wiki/Adjective "Adjective") expressing that its substantive is from or in that city or belongs to it.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-34)** The altitude of Mechelen, approximately in the middle of the central plain forming the large part of Flanders, is 7 m (23 ft) above sea level. Already closer to the higher southern Wallonia, the more eastern Leuven and Hasselt reach altitudes up to about 40 m (130 ft)
6. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-Note_Nationality-Minority-Education_45-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-Note_Nationality-Minority-Education_45-1) The relation between nationality, genetic ethnicity, native and mainly spoken language(s) (within a group of same ethnicity and age, in presence of elders, in ethnically mixed groups), and minority group identification, can be complex: Dutch nationals constituting one of the largest groups of foreigners, share the [standard language](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language") with Flemish locals but their accent is enough to immediately distinguish them. The majority of immigrants from certain other countries had belonged to a minority or disadvantaged group there. Children born in Belgium from residents of foreign nationality very often acquired Belgian citizenship. Regardless of nationality, according to Belgian Law, obligatory education in schools located in the Flemish Region are in the Dutch language. In Brussels, teaching is also done in French. The determination of statistical samples and interpretation of publicized figures can easily lead to false assumptions or conclusions.
